text: ‘The land must be divided up under these names’; it is not clear whether the names of tribes or of clans are meant. Nor is it clear how the two principles of apportionment by numbers and apportionment by lot were reconciled; presumably, lots were drawn between units whose numbers were approximately equal (as Benjamin and Nepthali).
The words ‘subdivided into’ are not found in the original but may be inferred from Ex. 6.16 sqq.
